title: pnpm install bootstraps the pnpm/Turborepo workspace
description: From a clean checkout, `pnpm install` (with the pnpm version pinned by `packageManager: pnpm@10.33.0` in `package.json`) bootstraps all 7 workspace members and exits 0. `node_modules/` exists at the root and inside every workspace member listed in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` (apps/website, apps/dashboard, packages/shared, packages/ui, packages/analytics, packages/auth, packages/forms). The .npmrc pins `node-linker=hoisted`, `package-import-method=copy`, `auto-install-peers=true`, and `strict-peer-dependencies=false`. Re-running `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` exits 0 against the committed pnpm-lock.yaml.
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` and confirm exit code 0 with no peer-dep failures.
Step 2: Verify each workspace member in pnpm-workspace.yaml has a populated node_modules/.
Step 3: Verify .npmrc pins node-linker=hoisted, package-import-method=copy, auto-install-peers=true, strict-peer-dependencies=false.

title: Bounded-context re-export pattern: src/app/ holds thin re-exports only
description: Every page under `apps/website/src/app/[locale]/*/page.tsx` and `apps/dashboard/src/app/[locale]/*/page.tsx` is a 1-3 line thin re-export that delegates to a context's `presentation/pages/<name>-page.tsx`. Every API route under `apps/dashboard/src/app/api/*/route.ts` is a thin re-export that delegates to a context's `api/<name>-handler.ts`. Cross-context imports use direct deep paths â€” context `index.ts` barrel files do not exist in either app. `next.config.mjs` for both apps configures `optimizePackageImports` for Clerk, lucide-react, and every internal `@causeflow/*` package.
steps: Step 1: Pick 3 random `apps/website/src/app/[locale]/**/page.tsx` files and confirm each is a single-line re-export.
Step 2: Pick 3 random `apps/dashboard/src/app/api/**/route.ts` files and confirm each is a single-line re-export.
Step 3: Confirm no `index.ts` barrel file exists under either `apps/website/src/contexts/` or `apps/dashboard/src/contexts/`.

title: 9 bounded contexts (investigation, approvals, audit, identity, onboarding, team, integrations, billing, settings) + shared with DDD layers
description: The dashboard's `src/contexts/` directory contains 10 bounded contexts: investigation, approvals, audit, identity, onboarding, team, integrations, billing, settings, shared. Each context has a DDD layout with only the layers it needs (domain, application, infrastructure, presentation, api). The website's `src/contexts/` directory contains 3 contexts: marketing, legal, shell. `index.ts` barrel files do not exist in any context in either app â€” all imports use direct deep paths. Per-context i18n files live at `infrastructure/i18n/{en,pt-br}.json` and are deep-merged by `apps/{website,dashboard}/src/lib/i18n/compose.ts`.
steps: Step 1: `ls apps/dashboard/src/contexts/` shows investigation, approvals, audit, identity, onboarding, team, integrations, billing, settings, shared (10 contexts).
Step 2: `ls apps/website/src/contexts/` shows marketing, legal, shell (3 contexts).
Step 3: Confirm no `index.ts` exists in any of those context directories.
implementation_notes: Verify-first zero-diff checkpoint: structure verified at real boundaries (dashboard /api/health HTTP 200 exercising shared/api re-export; dashboard compose.test.ts deep-merges all 10 context i18n files; website / HTTP 200 renders i18n via 3-context compose). Dashboard=10 contexts, website=3 contexts, no index.ts barrels, per-context i18n at infrastructure/i18n/{en,pt-br}.json deep-merged by lib/i18n/compose.ts in both apps.
qa_notes: Independent QA pass: dashboard src/contexts/ = exactly 10 dirs (approvals, audit, billing, identity, integrations, investigation, onboarding, settings, shared, team). website src/contexts/ = exactly 3 dirs (legal, marketing, shell). find apps/{dashboard,website}/src/contexts -name index.ts returns no barrel files. Each context uses only needed DDD layers (domain/application/infrastructure/presentation/api); shared also has a documented lib/ utility dir. Per-context i18n en.json+pt-br.json exist for all 10 dashboard + 3 website contexts. Both apps/src/lib/i18n/compose.ts deep-merge via @causeflow/shared/domain/utils/deep-merge. All AC-028 steps pass.

title: SST v3: each app's sst.config.ts provisions CloudFront + WAF + Lambda + Route 53 + ACM
description: `apps/website/sst.config.ts` and `apps/dashboard/sst.config.ts` each provision: a CloudFront distribution, a WAF WebACL (must live in `us-east-1` for CloudFront association â€” a separate `us-east-1` provider is created in each config), the Next.js compute (S3 + CloudFront + Lambda@Edge via OpenNext for the website; Lambda@Edge for the dashboard), Route 53 records, and an ACM certificate. The dashboard's config also provisions CloudWatch alarms for Lambda error rate and 5xx rate in production only. Domains: causeflow.ai / staging.causeflow.ai (website), dashboard.causeflow.ai / dashboard-staging.causeflow.ai (dashboard). The hosted zone is `Z01593322DGY9I94W9S7C`.
steps: Step 1: Confirm `apps/website/sst.config.ts` and `apps/dashboard/sst.config.ts` each declare a `us-east-1` provider for the WAF WebACL.
Step 2: Confirm both configs use `sst.aws.Nextjs` for the website and dashboard deployments.
Step 3: Confirm the dashboard config provisions CloudWatch alarms and the website config does not.

title: Auth.js v5 + Cognito: packages/auth/ retained for reference; only UserRole type re-export is consumed by the dashboard
description: `packages/auth/` retains the legacy Auth.js v5 (`next-auth@5.0.0-beta.30`) + `@auth/core` + `@aws-sdk/client-cognito-identity-provider` wiring at `packages/auth/src/infrastructure/auth-config.ts` for historical reference. The dashboard does not import the runtime exports from this package at runtime; the only consumer is the `UserRole` type re-export (`@causeflow/auth/types`) used by `apps/dashboard/src/contexts/identity/domain/rbac/__tests__/role-guard.test.ts`. The dev credentials provider auto-enables in development only; the production build strips it.
steps: Step 1: Confirm `packages/auth/package.json` and `packages/auth/src/infrastructure/auth-config.ts` exist.
Step 2: `grep -r "from '@causeflow/auth" apps/dashboard/src` returns only the `__tests__/role-guard.test.ts` import of the `UserRole` type.
Step 3: Confirm `apps/dashboard/next.config.mjs` lists `@aws-sdk/client-cognito-identity-provider` in `serverExternalPackages` so the AWS SDK stays server-side.

title: AWS SDK v3 limited to tenant-provisioning-fallback.ts and delete-user.ts CLI
description: The dashboard's runtime never holds AWS credentials. `@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb` and `@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb` are imported by exactly two paths: (1) `apps/dashboard/src/contexts/identity/infrastructure/tenant-provisioning-fallback.ts`, which writes a `Tenant` and `BillingAccount` directly to the Core API's DynamoDB table when the Core's `POST /v1/tenants` returns 403 â€” a documented temporary workaround; (2) `apps/dashboard/scripts/delete-user.ts`, which scans and batch-deletes every item in the Core's DynamoDB table scoped to a tenantId, and also cancels the corresponding Stripe customer. The provisioning fallback is called from `complete-profile-handler.ts` on a 403 from Core; the CLI script is gated by an explicit `--yes` flag. `AWS_REGION` and AWS credentials are read from the local shell env (or IAM role in prod) and are not part of the dashboard's runtime env.
steps: Step 1: `grep -r "@aws-sdk" apps/dashboard/src` returns only the tenant-provisioning-fallback import.
Step 2: `grep -r "@aws-sdk" apps/dashboard/scripts` returns the delete-user import.
Step 3: Confirm `apps/dashboard/.env.example` does not list `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` or `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` (these come from the shell env or SSO, not the dashboard's runtime env).

title: Loops.so declared as planned integration; CSP allow-list but no current consumer in this codebase
description: Loops.so is declared as a planned email-marketing integration in docs and the `apps/website/.env.example` (LOOPS_API_KEY). There is no `apps/website/src/app/api/notify` route in the current codebase â€” `apps/website/src/app` has no `/api` directory. The only runtime reference to Loops is the `Content-Security-Policy` `connect-src` allow-list at `apps/website/next.config.mjs` (https://app.loops.so). The integration has no current consumer; it is a planned integration only.
steps: Step 1: Confirm `apps/website/src/app` has no `/api` subdirectory.
Step 2: Confirm `apps/website/next.config.mjs` lists `https://app.loops.so` in CSP connect-src.
Step 3: Confirm `LOOPS_API_KEY` is listed in `apps/website/.env.example` but no code in `apps/website/src/` imports from any loops-related module.

title: pnpm turbo dev brings up website on $PORT (default 3000) and dashboard on $PORT+1 (3001) in parallel
description: After `pnpm install`, `pnpm turbo dev` starts both apps in parallel with content-hash caching. The website answers on the port passed in $PORT (default 3000) and the dashboard on $PORT+1 (default 3001). `curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:${PORT}` returns 200 for the website homepage and `curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:$((PORT+1))` returns 200 for the dashboard landing page. The dev process stays in the foreground until killed.
steps: Step 1: Run `PORT=3000 pnpm turbo dev` and tail `dev.log`.
Step 2: Poll `curl http://localhost:3000/` until it returns 200; then poll `curl http://localhost:3001/` until 200.
Step 3: Kill the dev process and confirm the script reports "Ready" once for each port.

title: pnpm turbo check-types runs tsc --noEmit across every workspace project
description: `pnpm turbo check-types` runs `tsc --noEmit` (dashboard uses `tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.build.json` to skip the Clerk type-test files) across all 7 workspace members in dependency order, exits 0, and prints a per-task success summary. The root `tsconfig.base.json` enables `strict: true` plus `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` and `moduleResolution: bundler`. Re-running it after intentionally introducing a type error inside `packages/shared/src/lib/example.ts` makes the command exit non-zero with a TS2xxx error.
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm turbo check-types` and confirm exit 0 with every task reporting "successful".
Step 2: Confirm `tsconfig.base.json` enables strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, moduleResolution=bundler.
Step 3: Confirm `apps/dashboard/tsconfig.build.json` is referenced so Clerk type tests are excluded.

title: pnpm exec biome check . runs the unified lint+format check; pnpm exec biome check --write . auto-fixes
description: Biome 2.4.4 (`biome.json` at the repo root) is the single lint+format tool. `pnpm exec biome check .` exits 0 against a clean tree. Introducing a malformed import (e.g. an ungrouped import that should be Biome-sorted) makes `pnpm exec biome check .` exit non-zero with a specific BIOME-### violation. `pnpm exec biome check --write .` re-runs the same check, auto-fixes the violation, and exits 0 on the same tree. ESLint and Prettier configs do not exist anywhere in the repo.
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm exec biome check .` and confirm exit 0.
Step 2: Confirm `biome.json` exists at the repo root and Biome 2.4.4 is in devDependencies.
Step 3: Confirm no .eslintrc, .prettierrc, or eslint.config.* file exists anywhere under the repo.
implementation_notes: Verify-first zero-diff checkpoint: pnpm exec biome check . exits 0 (76 warnings, 0 errors). biome.json at repo root with schema 2.4.4, @biomejs/biome ^2.4.4 installed as 2.4.4. No ESLint/Prettier configs found anywhere in repo. All AC-004 steps verified independently at real boundary.
qa_notes: Independent re-verification: Step 1 - pnpm exec biome check . exit=0, 76 warnings (all warn-level: noNonNullAssertion, noExplicitAny, noArrayIndexKey, useExhaustiveDependencies), 0 errors. Step 2 - biome.json =https://biomejs.dev/schemas/2.4.4/schema.json; @biomejs/biome@^2.4.4 in devDependencies, installed v2.4.4. Step 3 - find . -path ./node_modules -prune -o \( -name .eslintrc* -o -name .prettierrc* -o -name eslint.config.* -o -name prettier.config.* \) returns zero results. The orchestrator-claimed feature_list.json formatter error does not reproduce on this tree. All AC-004 steps pass.

title: pnpm vitest run executes the 7-project test suite
description: The root `vitest.config.ts` defines 7 projects (shared, forms, analytics, auth, ui, website, dashboard) using a forks pool with a maximum of 3 workers. `pnpm vitest run` (also `pnpm turbo test`) executes the full suite, exits 0 when green, and prints per-project pass/fail counts. The dashboard project has a 15s per-test timeout; the others use the default.
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm vitest run` and confirm exit 0 with all 7 projects reporting a pass count.
Step 2: Confirm `vitest.config.ts` declares projects for shared, forms, analytics, auth, ui, website, dashboard.
Step 3: Confirm the dashboard project config sets a 15s per-test timeout.

title: pnpm exec playwright test tests/ runs the E2E suite against chromium
description: `pnpm exec playwright test tests/` runs the Playwright 1.58.2 E2E suite under `tests/` (audit.spec.ts and visual-functional.spec.ts) against chromium only, 4 viewports (mobile 375Ã—812, tablet 768Ã—1024, desktop 1280Ã—800, wide 1440Ã—900), workers 3, fully parallel, with trace/video/screenshot disabled. The `playwright.config.ts` webServer block auto-starts the production server on port 3000 if not already running. The suite blocks analytics + tracker domains (google-analytics.com, *.clarity.ms, Intercom) in test.beforeEach.
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm exec playwright test tests/` and confirm exit 0 with a green report.
Step 2: Confirm `playwright.config.ts` configures chromium-only, 3 workers, fullyParallel, 4 projects for the 4 viewports.
Step 3: Confirm test.beforeEach in `tests/audit.spec.ts` and `tests/visual-functional.spec.ts` routes requests to analytics / clarity / Intercom domains through `page.route()` blocks.

title: pnpm turbo build builds all 7 packages and apps in dependency order
description: `pnpm turbo build` (root `turbo.json` with `build: { cache: true }`) runs the build for every workspace member in topological dependency order with content-hash caching, exits 0, and emits per-task success output. The website build produces a static export served from CloudFront + S3; the dashboard build runs `tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.build.json` and emits a Next.js Lambda@Edge bundle. After the build, `.next/` exists at the root of both apps.
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm turbo build` and confirm exit 0 with every task successful.
Step 2: Confirm `.next/` exists under `apps/website/` and `apps/dashboard/` after the build.
Step 3: Confirm `turbo.json` sets `build: { cache: true }` and the dashboard's build script invokes `tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.build.json`.

title: Mock-mode fallback: blank CORE_API_URL falls through to the mock Core API client
description: `apps/dashboard/src/lib/api/get-api-client.ts` resolves to `mock-api-client.ts` when `CORE_API_URL` is unset, and to `http-api-client.ts` otherwise. With `CORE_API_URL` blank in `apps/dashboard/.env.local`, `pnpm --filter dashboard dev` starts and every page that calls `getApiClient()` (analyses, billing, settings, team, integrations, audit, intelligence, topology, memory, notifications, approvals) renders against the mock client without contacting any external service. Setting `CORE_API_URL` to a non-existent host instead causes the same pages to fail-fast with a fetch error.
steps: Step 1: With `CORE_API_URL` blank, start `pnpm --filter dashboard dev` (port 3001) and load `/dashboard`.
Step 2: Confirm every navigation renders without a network error in the dev log.
Step 3: Set `CORE_API_URL=http://127.0.0.1:1` and confirm the same navigation surfaces a fetch failure in the dev log.

title: Per-context i18n files are deep-merged by apps/website/src/lib/i18n/compose.ts
description: Each website context owns its translations at `apps/website/src/contexts/{marketing,legal,shell}/infrastructure/i18n/{en,pt-br}.json`. `apps/website/src/lib/i18n/compose.ts` deep-merges all per-context JSON files into a single next-intl message tree at build time. Editing `marketing/infrastructure/i18n/en.json` to add a new key (e.g. `home.hero.cta`) and referencing it via `useTranslations('home.hero')` on the homepage renders the new key; the change is reflected on both `http://localhost:3000/` and `http://localhost:3000/pt-br/` (the latter reads `pt-br.json`).
steps: Step 1: Confirm the per-context i18n files exist for marketing, legal, and shell in both en.json and pt-br.json.
Step 2: Confirm `apps/website/src/lib/i18n/compose.ts` deep-merges the per-context files.
Step 3: Add a sentinel key to one of the per-context files, rebuild / restart dev, and confirm it surfaces on the matching page in both EN and PT-BR.

title: Sentry: 3 beforeSend hooks (server/edge/client) scrub PII; SDK no-ops when DSN is blank
description: `apps/dashboard/instrumentation.ts` registers the server (`sentry.server.config.ts`) and edge (`sentry.edge.config.ts`) configs; `apps/dashboard/instrumentation-client.ts` registers the client config. All three `beforeSend` hooks apply identical PII scrubbing (auth headers, cookies, request bodies, Clerk tokens) for SOC2 / GDPR / LGPD compliance. `onRequestError` forwards every Next.js error to Sentry. `src/app/global-error.tsx` is the root client error boundary that also calls `Sentry.captureException` in `useEffect`. When `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` is blank, the SDK no-ops at init and no network call is made.
steps: Step 1: Confirm `instrumentation.ts`, `sentry.server.config.ts`, `sentry.edge.config.ts`, `instrumentation-client.ts`, and `global-error.tsx` exist and the three Sentry.*.init() calls reference the same PII scrubbing helper.
Step 2: With `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN=` empty, load the dashboard and confirm no outbound request to `*.sentry.io`.
Step 3: Set a non-empty DSN, throw a synthetic error, and confirm the error lands in the Sentry project `causeflow-dashboard` under org `causeflow-ai`.

title: Vitest 7 projects with forks pool, max 3 workers, 15s per-test timeout on the dashboard project
description: The root `vitest.config.ts` defines 7 projects (shared, forms, analytics, auth, ui, website, dashboard) with a `pool: 'forks'` worker model and `poolOptions: { forks: { maxForks: 3 } }`. The dashboard project sets `testTimeout: 15000`; the other projects use the default. `pnpm vitest run` runs all 7 projects and emits a per-project pass/fail summary. `apps/dashboard/package.json` lists `vitest` as a dev dependency.
steps: Step 1: Confirm `vitest.config.ts` defines projects for shared, forms, analytics, auth, ui, website, dashboard.
Step 2: Confirm `pool: 'forks'` and `poolOptions: { forks: { maxForks: 3 } }` are set.
Step 3: Confirm the dashboard project has `testTimeout: 15000` and the others use the default.

title: pino structured JSON logs in apps/dashboard/src/lib/logger.ts; never logs raw tokens or PII
description: `apps/dashboard/src/lib/logger.ts` exports a pino 10.3.1 logger configured for structured JSON output. The logger redacts auth headers, Clerk session tokens, and Stripe secrets. `withAuth` and every API handler that catches a non-recoverable error also call `dashLogger.error` with a structured payload (method, path, userId, tenantId, duration) and forward the error to Sentry. The Sentry `beforeSend` hooks apply the same redaction in the observability layer.
steps: Step 1: Confirm `apps/dashboard/src/lib/logger.ts` exports a pino logger and `apps/dashboard/package.json` lists `pino@^10.3.1` as a dependency.

Step 2: Trigger a logged error in a dashboard API route and confirm the dev log shows structured JSON with method / path / userId / tenantId, and no JWT, Authorization header, or Stripe secret in the payload.
Step 3: Confirm the Sentry beforeSend hooks in instrumentation-client.ts, sentry.server.config.ts, and sentry.edge.config.ts apply the same redaction.

title: 9 EN routes return 200 and mirror under /pt-br/
description: The website exposes 9 EN routes (/, /product, /security, /integrations, /pricing, /use-cases, /from-opsgenie, /privacy, /terms) each returning HTTP 200. The same 9 routes are mirrored under /pt-br/ (so /pt-br/, /pt-br/product, ... /pt-br/terms) and return 200 with the Portuguese content. Each page renders the localized content from the per-context i18n files at `apps/website/src/contexts/{marketing,legal,shell}/infrastructure/i18n/pt-br.json`.
steps: Step 1: With the dev server up, `for path in / /product /security /integrations /pricing /use-cases /from-opsgenie /privacy /terms; do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "$path %{http_code}\n" http://localhost:3000$path; done` and confirm every line ends in 200.
Step 2: Repeat with `http://localhost:3000/pt-br$path` and confirm every line ends in 200.
Step 3: Confirm the response body for `/pricing` vs `/pt-br/pricing` contains different language (e.g. English vs Portuguese copy).

title: Website middleware pipeline: crawler detection â†’ staging auth â†’ geo-redirect â†’ Accept-Language â†’ NEXT_LOCALE cookie
description: `apps/website/src/middleware.ts` runs the documented pipeline. (1) Crawler detection: 45+ bot user-agent substrings (googlebot, bingbot, slurp, ...) never get redirected. A request with `User-Agent: Googlebot/2.1` to `/` returns 200 with the canonical EN URL, not a geo-redirect. (2) Staging auth: when `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_STAGE === 'staging'`, requests without the staging cookie get bounced to `/staging-auth`. (3) Geo-redirect: a request with `Cloudfront-Viewer-Country: BR` to `/` returns a 307 to `/pt-br/`. (4) Accept-Language: a request with `Accept-Language: pt-BR,en;q=0.8` to `/` returns 307 to `/pt-br/`. (5) NEXT_LOCALE cookie: a request with the cookie set to `pt-br` to `/` returns 307 to `/pt-br/`, and the cookie carries `Max-Age=31536000`.
steps: Step 1: Send `curl -H 'User-Agent: Googlebot/2.1' http://localhost:3000/` and confirm the response is 200, not a redirect.
Step 2: Send `curl -H 'Cloudfront-Viewer-Country: BR' http://localhost:3000/` and confirm a 307 to /pt-br/.

Step 3: Send `curl -H 'Accept-Language: pt-BR,en;q=0.8' -i http://localhost:3000/` and confirm a 307 to /pt-br/; then re-send with `Cookie: NEXT_LOCALE=pt-br` and confirm 307 with `Set-Cookie: NEXT_LOCALE=pt-br; Max-Age=31536000`.

title: Website security headers: X-Frame-Options DENY, CSP, HSTS 2y+preload, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy
description: Every response from the website (including SSG pages) carries: `X-Frame-Options: DENY`; a `Content-Security-Policy` that allow-lists `self`, GA4 (`https://www.googletagmanager.com`, `https://www.google-analytics.com`), Microsoft Clarity (`https://*.clarity.ms`), and Loops (`https://app.loops.so`); `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload`; `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`; and a `Permissions-Policy` that disables camera, microphone, and geolocation. Headers are defined in `apps/website/next.config.mjs` and apply to every route.
steps: Step 1: `curl -I http://localhost:3000/` and confirm every required header is present with the documented value.
Step 2: Confirm `apps/website/next.config.mjs` defines the headers() block with the documented CSP, HSTS, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy values.
Step 3: Send the same request to `/pt-br/` and confirm the header set is identical.

title: /get-started is retired; redirects to the dashboard's real sign-up URL per stage
description: `/get-started` is no longer a valid route in the website. `next.config.mjs` declares a `redirects()` block that maps `/get-started` (and `/pt-br/get-started`) to the per-stage sign-up URL: production â†’ `https://dashboard.causeflow.ai/auth/sign-up`; staging â†’ `https://dashboard-staging.causeflow.ai/auth/sign-up`. `curl -I http://localhost:3000/get-started` returns 307/308 with a Location header pointing at the dashboard sign-up URL.
steps: Step 1: `curl -I http://localhost:3000/get-started` and confirm a 307/308 with Location set to the dashboard sign-up URL.
Step 2: Confirm the same redirect applies to `/pt-br/get-started`.
Step 3: Confirm `apps/website/next.config.mjs` contains a redirects() entry keyed on `/get-started`.

title: /staging-auth is the password gate for the staging deploy (STAGING_PASSWORD=causeflow-staging-2026)
description: When the website is deployed to staging, requests to any non-asset URL without the `staging-authorized` cookie are redirected to `/staging-auth`, which renders a password form. Submitting the documented password `causeflow-staging-2026` (set in `apps/website/sst.config.ts`) sets the staging-authorized cookie and redirects back to the originally-requested URL. In local dev (`NEXT_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_STAGE !== 'staging'`), the gate is bypassed.
steps: Step 1: With `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_STAGE=staging` set, `curl -I http://localhost:3000/` and confirm a 307 to `/staging-auth`.
Step 2: Submit the documented password and confirm the staging-authorized cookie is set and the original URL is followed.
Step 3: Confirm the password is read from `apps/website/sst.config.ts` (production + staging) and that local dev bypasses the gate.

title: robots.ts (noindex on staging) + sitemap.ts (XML for all routesÃ—locales) + structured-data.tsx (JSON-LD)
description: `apps/website/src/app/robots.ts` returns a robots response that disallows crawling (`Disallow: /`) when `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_STAGE === 'staging'` and allows it otherwise. `apps/website/src/app/sitemap.ts` generates an XML sitemap that includes every route Ã— locale (EN + PT-BR). `apps/website/src/contexts/marketing/presentation/components/sections/structured-data.tsx` injects JSON-LD blocks (Organization, Website, FAQPage) into the home and product pages â€” `curl http://localhost:3000/` includes `<script type="application/ld+json">` payloads for each schema.
steps: Step 1: `curl http://localhost:3000/robots.txt` and confirm a `Disallow: /` line on staging or an empty/allow on production.
Step 2: `curl http://localhost:3000/sitemap.xml` and confirm an XML payload with one entry per route Ã— locale.
Step 3: `curl http://localhost:3000/` and grep for `<script type="application/ld+json">`; confirm the payload parses to JSON with `@type: Organization`.

title: GA4 + Microsoft Clarity loaded via AnalyticsProvider; no-op when env vars are empty
description: `packages/analytics/src/infrastructure/ga4/index.ts` exposes `initGA4` and `trackGA4PageView`; `packages/analytics/src/infrastructure/clarity/index.ts` exposes `initClarity`. The AnalyticsProvider mounted by `apps/website/src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` calls both when `NEXT_PUBLIC_GA4_MEASUREMENT_ID` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLARITY_ID` are set, and is a no-op when they are blank. With both env vars empty, the homepage does not make any request to `https://www.google-analytics.com` or `https://www.clarity.ms` (verifiable via Playwright network logging). When set, the page emits a gtag config call and a Clarity init call on first paint.

title: GitHub Actions: 2 workflows (website-deploy.yml, dashboard-deploy.yml) with frozen-lockfile + type-check + lint + test + build + SST deploy

title: RBAC: 9 permissions Ã— 2 roles enforced at API level (withAuth) and component level (RoleGuard)
description: `apps/dashboard/src/contexts/identity/domain/rbac/permissions.ts` defines 9 permissions (MANAGE_TEAM, MANAGE_INTEGRATIONS, MANAGE_BILLING, MANAGE_SETTINGS, SUBMIT_ANALYSIS, VIEW_ANALYSES, VIEW_INTEGRATIONS, VIEW_BILLING, VIEW_SETTINGS, VIEW_TEAM) and the `hasPermission(role, permission)` predicate. The role map assigns all 9 to `admin` and 5 read/submit permissions to `member`. `withAuth(handler, { adminOnly: true })` returns 403 for member; `withAuth(handler, { requiredRole: 'admin' })` does the same. `RoleGuard` + the `usePermission` hook hide admin-only UI controls for members. `requirePermission(role, perm)` throws server-side before the handler runs.

title: Credits system: free plan = 3 credits/month lazy-renewed every 30 days; 402 CREDITS_EXHAUSTED on exhaustion; plans.ts is the single source of truth
description: `packages/shared/src/domain/constants/plans.ts` is the single source of truth for plan catalog (starter=15, pro=60, business=200 credits/month, enterprise=unlimited). The free plan grants 3 credits/month, renewed lazily on the next `GET /api/metrics` call once 30 days have elapsed. Submitting a new analysis decrements by 1; when credits reach 0, `POST /api/incidents` returns `402` with `{ code: 'CREDITS_EXHAUSTED' }`. Credits stay active during `canceling` status until `currentPeriodEnd`.
steps: Step 1: Confirm `packages/shared/src/domain/constants/plans.ts` defines the documented plan catalog and `getCreditsForPlan()` returns the documented counts.

Step 3: As a free-plan tenant with 1 credit remaining, `POST /api/incidents` returns 200 and a subsequent call returns 402 CREDITS_EXHAUSTED.

title: withAuth: 401/403/429 enforcement at the API boundary
description: `apps/dashboard/src/lib/api/with-auth.ts#withAuth(handler, options)` enforces, in order: (1) Clerk auth â€” no session returns 401 with `{ error: 'Authentication required.' }`; (2) tenant validation â€” no `orgId` returns 403 with `{ error: 'Profile setup required. Please complete onboarding.' }` (skippable with `allowNoOrg: true`); (3) role guard â€” `adminOnly: true` or `requiredRole: 'admin'` returns 403 with `{ error: 'Admin access required.' }` / `{ error: 'Insufficient role for this action.' }`; (4) per-user rate limit â€” 60 req/min default, 429 with `Retry-After` header on excess; (5) staff-only â€” `staffOnly: true` returns 403 for non-staff emails. The wrapped handler runs only after all guards pass.
steps: Step 1: With no session, `GET /api/settings` returns 401 with the documented body.
Step 2: With a session but no `orgId`, `GET /api/settings` returns 403 with the documented body.
Step 3: With a member session, `POST /api/billing/checkout` (adminOnly) returns 403; with 70 requests in 60s the 70th returns 429 with a `Retry-After` header.

title: BFF pattern: Clerk session JWT forwarded as Bearer to Core API; never exposed to the browser
description: `apps/dashboard/src/lib/api/get-backend-token.ts` reads the Clerk session JWT server-side via `getToken()` from `@clerk/nextjs/server`. The dashboard's `http-api-client.ts` attaches it as `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` on every Core API request. The browser never sees the token: the React tree only renders Core API responses, never the JWT itself. The HTTP client also strips auth headers from any error that reaches `Sentry.captureException`. The dashboard's `.env.local` lists no `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` or any other Core-side secret â€” only `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` and `CLERK_SECRET_KEY`.
steps: Step 1: Sign in, then in the browser dev tools confirm no `__session` JWT, no `Authorization` header on outbound requests, and no JWT in any rendered HTML.
Step 2: Tail the dev log while loading a dashboard page and confirm every Core API call carries `Authorization: Bearer ...` server-side.
Step 3: Confirm `apps/dashboard/src/lib/api/http-api-client.ts` scrubs `Authorization` from any error it forwards to Sentry.

title: Plan gate: active only when status âˆˆ {active, trialing} AND currentPeriodEnd is set
description: `apps/dashboard/src/contexts/billing/application/plan-status.ts#fetchPlanStatus` returns `hasActivePlan: true` only when the Core API's `GET /v1/billing/subscription` returns `status âˆˆ {active, trialing}` AND `currentPeriodEnd` is set. Fresh tenants whose Core subscription has `status='active'` but no `currentPeriodEnd` (because they have never completed Stripe checkout) return `hasActivePlan: false`, which the dashboard layout uses to render the plan gate. The Core API default of `status='active'` for never-subscribed tenants is overridden by the missing `currentPeriodEnd` check.
steps: Step 1: As a fresh tenant with Core returning `{ status: 'active', currentPeriodEnd: null }`, `getPlanStatus()` returns `hasActivePlan: false` and the dashboard layout renders the plan gate.
Step 2: As a paid tenant with Core returning `{ status: 'active', currentPeriodEnd: '2026-12-31T...' }`, `getPlanStatus()` returns `hasActivePlan: true` and the dashboard renders.
Step 3: Confirm `plan-status.ts` documents the cross-check rationale (the Core API defaults to active for fresh tenants).

title: Live SSE feed: incident detail pages forward the Core API's streaming response

The Clerk middleware does not block streaming responses; the dashboard's API route handler returns a `Response` with `Content-Type: text/event-stream` and pipes Core's events through. A running investigation emits per-agent events (log, metric, change, code, infra, db) to the client as they complete.

Step 3: Confirm the Clerk middleware's `isPublicRoute` matcher does not block the streaming endpoint and the API route returns a streaming Response, not a buffered JSON one.

title: lvh.me loopback rewrite: localhost â†’ lvh.me for Stripe callbacks to bypass the Core WAF SSRF filter
description: Core's WAF rejects Stripe callbacks containing `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` in the URL. The dashboard's checkout and portal handlers rewrite the URL host from `localhost` to `lvh.me` (a public-DNS loopback alias) before calling Core. The dev middleware (`apps/dashboard/src/middleware.ts`) does the inverse rewrite â€” a request with hostname `lvh.me` in dev is redirected to `localhost` so the Clerk session cookie is honored. Production uses the real dashboard hostname; the rewrite is dev-only.
steps: Step 1: Confirm `apps/dashboard/src/middleware.ts` redirects `lvh.me` â†’ `localhost` in dev.
Step 2: Confirm the checkout / portal handlers rewrite `localhost` â†’ `lvh.me` before calling Core.
Step 3: In dev, set `CORE_API_URL=http://api.lvh.me:9999` (or similar) and confirm the handlers' outbound URL uses the lvh.me host.

description: `apps/dashboard/src/middleware.ts` no longer imports `clerkMiddleware`. The middleware reads the `__session` httpOnly cookie (set by the dashboard's sign-in route handler), verifies it with `JWT_SECRET` using the same `jose` library the Core uses, and lets the request through. `withAuth(handler, options)` extracts the cookie, calls the Core's `GET /api/v1/whoami` with `Authorization: Bearer `, and resolves `userId` + `tenantId` + `role` from the response. The dashboard's `/auth/sign-in` and `/auth/sign-up` pages are local React forms that POST to a new dashboard route handler (`/api/auth/login`, `/api/auth/register`) which proxies to the Core's endpoints, then sets the `__session` cookie. An unauthenticated `GET /dashboard` still redirects to `/auth/sign-in` (preserving AC-019's behavior).

description: `apps/website/sst.config.ts` and `apps/dashboard/sst.config.ts` are deleted. The `withSentryConfig(...)` wrapper in `apps/dashboard/next.config.mjs` is removed (the surrounding `next.config.mjs` keeps its `optimizePackageImports`, headers, redirects, and image allow-list). `.github/workflows/website-deploy.yml` and `.github/workflows/dashboard-deploy.yml` keep their `check-types` / `lint` / `test` / `build` steps but drop the `sst deploy` step (replaced with a comment pointing operators to the Docker build). Both apps have a multi-stage `Dockerfile` that mirrors the `relay` subproject's pattern: a builder stage that runs `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` + `pnpm build`, then a runtime stage that runs `pnpm start`. The `docker-compose.yml` from AC-044 references those Dockerfiles.

description: The OSS dashboard (or settings surface it proxies) can select Core's investigation LLM connector. Default is Ornith 9B on `:8081`. When Ornith's context window is insufficient for the investigation (documented overflow / context-too-large signal from Core), the operator switches to **DeepSeek V4 Flash** via OpenCode Go (`opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free`) or NVIDIA NIM (`deepseek-ai/deepseek-v4-pro` or `deepseek-v4-flash`). Credentials are supplied at runtime from the operator host (`~/.config/opencode`, `~/.pi/agent/auth.json`, or env vars derived from them) and are never committed to the repo. After selection, Core health/settings reflect the active connector.
